fields.summary: Support multiple attempts on the node when AMRMProxy is enabled
Enable YARN RM scale out via federation using multiple RM's

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: Currently {{AMRMProxy}} initializes an interceptor chain pipeline for every active AM in the node but it doesn't clean up & reinitialize correctly if there's a second attempt for any AM in the same node. This jira is to track the changes required to support multiple attempts on the node when AMRMProxy is enabled.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Recreate interceptor chain for different attemptId in the same node in AMRMProxy

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

This message was automatically generated.
The failed test is not related to the patch.

[~giovanni.fumarola], I think we should reattach the new attempt to the same pipeline instead of shutting it down as otherwise all the spanned containers in the secondary _subclusters_ will now be killed.

Hi [~subru], today we don't support second attempt because the UAMs in the secondary subclusters fail when the first attempt fails. When the UAM client for the second attempt starts up, the application is already in failed state in secondary subclusters. 

What you suggested will work if the second attempt is in the same node as the first attempt. However the problem still exists if the second attempt is launched in a different node. I think we need to address this in UAM client in Federation Interceptor.

Thanks for your response [~botong]. Essentially there are 2 scenarios:
  1. Second attempt on same node - In this case, UAMs on secondaries should not fail. Instead they should simply re-register the _original_ attempt using the {{AMRMToken}}. In essence, for UAMs, the process is similar to RM failover.
  2. Second attempt on different node - YARN-6128 should address this use case.
Please see my comments in duplicate: https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-6282?focusedCommentId=15966160

Per offline discussion with [~subru]: 

The two cases (1,2) above can be handled uniformly by YARN-6128, but we can add later on some more efficient caching of {{AMRMToken}} for the (1) case. Please address the comments from duplicate, ok to postpone (1).

Thanks [~curino], [~subru] and [~botong].

About this feedback: https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-6282?focusedCommentId=15966160
I added the NPE check, and I double checked and I noticed we don't need a finally block to ensure we leave things tidy since it is already handled in the code.

The failed test is not related to the patch.

[~giovanni.fumarola], I think we should reattach the new attempt to the same pipeline instead of shutting it down as otherwise all the spanned containers in the secondary _subclusters_ will now be killed.

Hi [~subru], today we don't support second attempt because the UAMs in the secondary subclusters fail when the first attempt fails. When the UAM client for the second attempt starts up, the application is already in failed state in secondary subclusters. 

What you suggested will work if the second attempt is in the same node as the first attempt. However the problem still exists if the second attempt is launched in a different node. I think we need to address this in UAM client in Federation Interceptor.

Thanks for your response [~botong]. Essentially there are 2 scenarios:
  1. Second attempt on same node - In this case, UAMs on secondaries should not fail. Instead they should simply re-register the _original_ attempt using the {{AMRMToken}}. In essence, for UAMs, the process is similar to RM failover.
  2. Second attempt on different node - YARN-6128 should address this use case.
Please see my comments in duplicate: https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-6282?focusedCommentId=15966160

Per offline discussion with [~subru]: 

The two cases (1,2) above can be handled uniformly by YARN-6128, but we can add later on some more efficient caching of {{AMRMToken}} for the (1) case. Please address the comments from duplicate, ok to postpone (1).

Thanks [~curino], [~subru] and [~botong].

About this feedback: https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-6282?focusedCommentId=15966160
I added the NPE check, and I double checked and I noticed we don't need a finally block to ensure we leave things tidy since it is already handled in the code.
